prompt_version: WRITER_SYSTEM_PROMPT v1 — full thinking block, no constraints on length or drafting

episode_plan_input.hook: Kai is cleaning an empty back room alone when he opens a dusty cupboard and finds an old video camera with a tape still inside
key_events: Kai finds the camera in the cupboard and his curiosity takes over immediately — THEREFORE he presses play and watches the tape instead of working: the screen shows the same back room fifty years ago, and a man and a woman hide a small metal box under the floorboard next to the window, then cover it carefully

BUT Mrs Doran calls his name from the hallway — THEREFORE Kai stops the tape and hides the camera fast in his work bag, just before she walks in and asks him sharply why the room is not clean yet; Kai lies and says he is almost finished, and she tells him she is going to the van for more bags but will be back in ten minutes

THEREFORE the moment Mrs Doran leaves, Kai goes to the floorboard next to the window — BUT when he looks closely he sees fresh scratches around the edge of one board, scratches that are not old and dusty like everything else in the room; Kai does not stop to think about this and tries to lift the board with his fingers

The board is loose but Kai cannot open it with his hands alone — THEREFORE he stands up to get a tool from his bag, and at that exact moment he hears a sound: slow, heavy footsteps, walking directly above his head, from the locked floor upstairs
episode_plan_input.ending_hook: The house is supposed to be completely empty — so who is walking upstairs?

output:
THINKING BLOCK ONLY — prose never reached. Full thinking block consumed all 3500 tokens.

Key thinking block contents:
- Mapped 4 key events to scenes with paragraph estimates
- Solved solo-character dialogue problem by using Mrs Doran as antagonist (micro-conflict: she is suspicious/sharp, he is evasive/lying)
- Planned vocabulary target placement (two appearances each)
- Drafted full prose episode (~515 words) INSIDE the thinking block
- Caught one CEFR grammar violation: 'You have been in here for forty minutes' — present perfect, not allowed at A2. Proposed fix: 'You came in here forty minutes ago.'
- Counted words sentence-by-sentence — this is what consumed the token budget

Prose draft produced inside thinking block (excerpt):
'The room smelled of dust and old wood. Kai moved his mop slowly across the floor. The house was abandoned — empty for many years...'
[Full draft present in thinking block but clean prose output never reached]

observations.thinking_self_correction: The model caught a present perfect violation ('You have been in here for forty minutes') in its own draft and proposed a fix before writing the final output. This is valuable research data — the thinking block is functioning as a CEFR compliance checker. Research question: does the thinking block measurably improve CEFR compliance vs no thinking block? Worth testing with the classifier once it is built.
observations.word_counting: The model counted every sentence individually (e.g. 'The room smelled of dust and old wood. (8)'). This is not requested behaviour — it is the model's interpretation of the sentence length constraint. It consumed a large proportion of the token budget. Note: the classifier will handle measurement. We do not need the model to count. This behaviour should be noted but the thinking block should not be constrained yet (per Opus advice) — the self-correction value outweighs the token cost for now.
observations.token_budget: 3500 max_tokens insufficient for full thinking block + clean prose output. Increasing to 6000 for next run.
observations.prose_quality: Draft prose inside thinking block is strong — natural dialogue, clear dramatic irony (fresh scratches, footsteps), clean A2 vocabulary. Story is more developed than exp_001 baseline.
observations.next_steps: Re-run with max_tokens=6000 to get clean prose output after thinking block.
